# Review: `deepmind/621.lean` ‚Äî Erd≈ës Problem 621

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` on line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (line 19).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd≈ës Problem 621`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/621`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (triangle-independent and triangle-transversal edge sets).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[EGT96]` (Erd≈ës‚ÄìGallai‚ÄìTuza) and `[NoSu16]` (Norin‚ÄìSun).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos621 ... end Erdos621`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos621`).
- [x] `open SimpleGraph` appears **before** the namespace block (line 34).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `mkEdge` (abbrev, line 39) has a docstring.
- [x] `IsTriangleIndependent` (def, line 45) has a docstring.
- [x] `IsTriangleTransversal` (def, line 57) has a docstring.
- [x] No `noncomputable` needed ‚Äî definitions use decidable relations and are computable.
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- [x] Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `IsTriangleIndependent`, `IsTriangleTransversal`.
- [x] Value-level function uses `lowerCamelCase`: `mkEdge`.
- [x] No `local notation` used ‚Äî none needed for this problem.
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks on definitions. Optional but worth considering for `IsTriangleIndependent` and `IsTriangleTransversal` to verify expected behavior on small examples.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 76).
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_621` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]` (line 75).
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 63‚Äì74) describing the conjecture and its resolution.
- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[EGT96]` and `[NoSu16]`.

### `answer()` usage

- [x] **Case 3 applies.** The problem states a direct assertion ("Conjecture: Œ±‚ÇÅ(G) + œÑ‚ÇÅ(G) ‚â§ n¬≤/4"), so the theorem type is `P` directly with no `answer()` wrapper. This is correct.
- [x] No quantifiers improperly pulled out as theorem parameters with respect to `answer()` ‚Äî `answer()` is not used, so this constraint does not apply. The parameters `n`, `G`, `S`, `T` with maximality/minimality hypotheses are appropriate for expressing the bound.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research solved` ‚Äî valid category. The problem was proved by Norin and Sun (2016), so this is appropriate. The proof is not formally verified, justifying `research solved` over `research formally solved using ...`.
- [x] `research solved` with `sorry` in proof body is consistent (no linter conflict since the "open" linter only flags `research open` with sorry-free proofs).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5` (combinatorics).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (trivially, only one number).
- [x] No duplicates.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by` / `sorry` on lines 83‚Äì84 (multi-line format, which is acceptable per checklist ¬ß9).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches included (appropriate since the formal proof is not provided).
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A ‚Äî No variants present. The file formalizes a single conjecture.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚Ñï`, `‚àÄ`, `‚äÜ`, `‚àà`, `¬¨`, `‚àß`, `‚à®`, `‚Üí`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run. Structural inspection shows no obvious issues; `sorry` warnings expected from the open proof body.
- [ ] Custom linter compliance not verified via build. By inspection:
  - `linter.style.copyright`: Should pass (exact header present).
  - `linter.style.category_attribute`: Should pass (single valid category).
  - `linter.style.ams_attribute`: Should pass (single valid AMS tag, valid code).
  - `linter.style.answer_attribute`: Should pass (no `answer()` used; Case 3).
  - `linter.style.namespace`: Should pass (all declarations inside `Erdos621`).

---

## Summary

**Status: PASS**

The file is clean and conforms to all checklist requirements. The formalization correctly uses Case 3 (direct assertion, no `answer()`) since the Erd≈ës‚ÄìGallai‚ÄìTuza conjecture asserts a bound rather than asking a question. The category `research solved` is appropriate given the Norin‚ÄìSun proof (2016). All definitions have docstrings, naming conventions are followed, and the module docstring includes proper references. The only optional suggestion is adding `@[category test]` sanity-check examples for the custom definitions.

    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 621
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/621](https://www.erdosproblems.com/621)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA problem of Erd≈ës, Gallai, and Tuza on triangle-independent and triangle-transversal
    25‚Üíedge sets in graphs. Proved by Norin and Sun.
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[EGT96] Erd≈ës, P., Gallai, T., and Tuza, Z., _Covering and independence in triangle
    28‚Üístructures_, Discrete Mathematics 150 (1996), 89-101.
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí[NoSu16] Norin, S. and Sun, Y., _Triangle-independent sets vs. triangle-transversals_,
    31‚ÜíEuropean Journal of Combinatorics 56 (2016), 95-104.
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üínamespace Erdos621
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/-- Create an unordered pair from two vertices, representing an edge. -/
    39‚Üíabbrev mkEdge {V : Type*} (a b : V) : Sym2 V := Quot.mk _ (a, b)
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/--
    42‚ÜíA set of edges $S$ is triangle-independent in $G$ if $S \subseteq E(G)$ and every
    43‚Üítriangle in $G$ contains at most one edge from $S$.
    44‚Üí-/
    45‚Üídef IsTriangleIndependent {n : ‚Ñï} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj]
    46‚Üí    (S : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n))) : Prop :=
    47‚Üí  S ‚äÜ G.edgeFinset ‚àß
    48‚Üí  ‚àÄ a b c : Fin n, G.Adj a b ‚Üí G.Adj b c ‚Üí G.Adj a c ‚Üí
    49‚Üí    ¬¨(mkEdge a b ‚àà S ‚àß mkEdge b c ‚àà S) ‚àß
    50‚Üí    ¬¨(mkEdge a b ‚àà S ‚àß mkEdge a c ‚àà S) ‚àß
    51‚Üí    ¬¨(mkEdge b c ‚àà S ‚àß mkEdge a c ‚àà S)
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/--
    54‚ÜíA set of edges $T$ is a triangle transversal in $G$ if $T \subseteq E(G)$ and every
    55‚Üítriangle in $G$ contains at least one edge from $T$.
    56‚Üí-/
    57‚Üídef IsTriangleTransversal {n : ‚Ñï} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj]
    58‚Üí    (T : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n))) : Prop :=
    59‚Üí  T ‚äÜ G.edgeFinset ‚àß
    60‚Üí  ‚àÄ a b c : Fin n, G.Adj a b ‚Üí G.Adj b c ‚Üí G.Adj a c ‚Üí
    61‚Üí    mkEdge a b ‚àà T ‚à® mkEdge b c ‚àà T ‚à® mkEdge a c ‚àà T
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí/--
    64‚Üí**Erd≈ës Problem 621** (Erd≈ës-Gallai-Tuza conjecture) [EGT96]:
    65‚Üí
    66‚ÜíLet $G$ be a graph on $n$ vertices. Define $\alpha_1(G)$ as the maximum number of edges
    67‚Üíforming a set that contains at most one edge from every triangle, and $\tau_1(G)$ as
    68‚Üíthe minimum number of edges forming a set that contains at least one edge from
    69‚Üíevery triangle.
    70‚Üí
    71‚ÜíConjecture: $\alpha_1(G) + \tau_1(G) \leq n^2/4$.
    72‚Üí
    73‚ÜíThis was proved by Norin and Sun [NoSu16].
    74‚Üí-/
    75‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    76‚Üítheorem erdos_621 (n : ‚Ñï) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj]
    77‚Üí    (S : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n)))
    78‚Üí    (hS : IsTriangleIndependent G S)
    79‚Üí    (hS_max : ‚àÄ S' : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n)), IsTriangleIndependent G S' ‚Üí S'.card ‚â§ S.card)
    80‚Üí    (T : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n)))
    81‚Üí    (hT : IsTriangleTransversal G T)
    82‚Üí    (hT_min : ‚àÄ T' : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n)), IsTriangleTransversal G T' ‚Üí T.card ‚â§ T'.card) :
    83‚Üí    4 * (S.card + T.card) ‚â§ n ^ 2 := by
    84‚Üí  sorry
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üíend Erdos621
    87‚Üí

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 621

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/621](https://www.erdosproblems.com/621)

A problem of Erd≈ës, Gallai, and Tuza on triangle-independent and triangle-transversal
edge sets in graphs. Proved by Norin and Sun.

[EGT96] Erd≈ës, P., Gallai, T., and Tuza, Z., _Covering and independence in triangle
structures_, Discrete Mathematics 150 (1996), 89-101.

[NoSu16] Norin, S. and Sun, Y., _Triangle-independent sets vs. triangle-transversals_,
European Journal of Combinatorics 56 (2016), 95-104.
-/

open SimpleGraph

namespace Erdos621

/-- Create an unordered pair from two vertices, representing an edge. -/
abbrev mkEdge {V : Type*} (a b : V) : Sym2 V := Quot.mk _ (a, b)

/--
A set of edges $S$ is triangle-independent in $G$ if $S \subseteq E(G)$ and every
triangle in $G$ contains at most one edge from $S$.
-/
def IsTriangleIndependent {n : ‚Ñï} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj]
    (S : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n))) : Prop :=
  S ‚äÜ G.edgeFinset ‚àß
  ‚àÄ a b c : Fin n, G.Adj a b ‚Üí G.Adj b c ‚Üí G.Adj a c ‚Üí
    ¬¨(mkEdge a b ‚àà S ‚àß mkEdge b c ‚àà S) ‚àß
    ¬¨(mkEdge a b ‚àà S ‚àß mkEdge a c ‚àà S) ‚àß
    ¬¨(mkEdge b c ‚àà S ‚àß mkEdge a c ‚àà S)

/--
A set of edges $T$ is a triangle transversal in $G$ if $T \subseteq E(G)$ and every
triangle in $G$ contains at least one edge from $T$.
-/
def IsTriangleTransversal {n : ‚Ñï} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj]
    (T : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n))) : Prop :=
  T ‚äÜ G.edgeFinset ‚àß
  ‚àÄ a b c : Fin n, G.Adj a b ‚Üí G.Adj b c ‚Üí G.Adj a c ‚Üí
    mkEdge a b ‚àà T ‚à® mkEdge b c ‚àà T ‚à® mkEdge a c ‚àà T

/--
**Erd≈ës Problem 621** (Erd≈ës-Gallai-Tuza conjecture) [EGT96]:

Let $G$ be a graph on $n$ vertices. Define $\alpha_1(G)$ as the maximum number of edges
forming a set that contains at most one edge from every triangle, and $\tau_1(G)$ as
the minimum number of edges forming a set that contains at least one edge from
every triangle.

Conjecture: $\alpha_1(G) + \tau_1(G) \leq n^2/4$.

This was proved by Norin and Sun [NoSu16].
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_621 (n : ‚Ñï) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj]
    (S : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n)))
    (hS : IsTriangleIndependent G S)
    (hS_max : ‚àÄ S' : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n)), IsTriangleIndependent G S' ‚Üí S'.card ‚â§ S.card)
    (T : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n)))
    (hT : IsTriangleTransversal G T)
    (hT_min : ‚àÄ T' : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n)), IsTriangleTransversal G T' ‚Üí T.card ‚â§ T'.card) :
    4 * (S.card + T.card) ‚â§ n ^ 2 := by
  sorry

end Erdos621

content.text: The review status is **PASS**. No changes needed.
